meta.title: Global AI Pre-IPO Pipeline Pilot
meta.purpose: Track global private AI / AI supply-chain companies from early growth to pre-IPO, with IPO certainty, investor access, evidence and next actions.
meta.coverage: v3 expanded to broader global private AI market: prior two-round universe, Korea/Japan, InterVest, AI chips, apps, infra and robotics.

companies.name: Rebellions
companies.latestValuation: Media: $1.4B after Arm/Samsung Ventures round; later media indicates higher pre-IPO marks need verification
companies.latestFunding: Media: ~$250M Series C; additional pre-IPO reports unverified
ipoSignals: Korean media: KOSPI IPO preparation
Global IR ahead of IPO
Sapeon merger integration
Pre-IPO funding reports
companies.nextAction: Ask Samsung Ventures + InterVest for data room: revenue/backlog, KRX preliminary review status, lead underwriter, lock-up and valuation.

evidence.note: Official site positions RebelServer for sovereign/on-prem inference; lists Samsung/Arm/SK/KT/Aramco/AWS ecosystem logos.
RebelServer page claims on-prem sovereign AI, 2 PFLOPS FP8 with 8 RebelCards / Rebel100 chip; vLLM/PyTorch/Triton support.
DCD: valued at $1.4B following Arm and Samsung Ventures investment; Atom/Atom Max deployed in Japan, Saudi Arabia and US per company statement.

companies.notes: Core Korea target. Investment case hinges on converting AI-chip narrative into revenue/backlog and KRX visibility.
openQuestions: Has KRX preliminary listing review been submitted?

companies.name: FuriosaAI
companies.latestValuation: Media: seeking up to $500M / KRW 700B pre-IPO funding; valuation not confirmed
companies.latestFunding: Prior C round / pre-IPO reports; verify with InterVest and advisors
ipoSignals: Bloomberg/DCD/BusinessKorea: pre-IPO funding before IPO
Media reports: independent path after rejecting big tech acquisition
companies.nextAction: Ask InterVest whether $500M round closed, valuation, Morgan Stanley/Mirae role, RNGD production, top customers.

evidence.note: InterVest official portfolio lists Furiosa AI under Semiconductor.
Google News cluster: FuriosaAI seeks up to $500M / KRW 700B before IPO; needs primary verification.

companies.notes: High beta. Bigger technical/customer risk than Rebellions.

companies.latestValuation: Media: target IPO valuation around KRW 1T / ~$700M; verify

2026 IPO plan media reports
companies.nextAction: Check KRX filing, lead underwriter, Hyundai/Foxconn design-win conversion, revenue by robotics/ADAS/industrial vision.

evidence.note: Reuters/Google News: Korean AI chip startup DeepX prepares public share offering.

companies.notes: Clear IPO signal but smaller TAM/edge market fragmentation.

Unicorn funding headlines
companies.nextAction: Ask InterVest for ARR, major enterprise customers, IPO adviser, Japan/US expansion and gross margin.

evidence.note: InterVest official portfolio lists Upstage under AI.

companies.notes: Best Korean AI application candidate; less hardware risk.

companies.name: Suno
companies.latestValuation: Media: funding at ~$5B+ valuation; TechCrunch cluster: ~$300M ARR / 2M paid subscribers
companies.latestFunding: Media: new funding round at ~$5B+ valuation
ipoSignals: Large ARR and paid subscriber signals; no filing yet

redFlags: Label licensing economics may compress gross margin
openQuestions: Is reported $300M ARR audited/contracted or run-rate?
Copyright litigation status and settlement path?
companies.topInvestorSignal: $125M round reported with Lightspeed Venture Partners, Nat Friedman, Daniel Gross, Matrix, Founder Collective. Later $5B+ valuation/funding media signals need investor-list verification.

companies.name: Lovable
companies.subSector: Vibe coding / AI-native software builder / B2B app platform
companies.latestValuation: Media: $6.6B valuation after $330M/€281M Series B; ARR/run-rate claims $400-500M need verification
companies.latestFunding: Series A: $200M led by Accel at $1.8B valuation; Series B media: $330M / $6.6B with CapitalG, Menlo, Nvidia/Alphabet VC arms
companies.topInvestorSignal: Accel led $200M Series A; existing investors included 20VC, byFounders, Creandum, Hummingbird, Visionaries Club. Series B media: CapitalG and Menlo Ventures, with Nvidia/Alphabet VC arms reported.
investors: angel investors incl. Sebastian Siemiatkowski/Stewart Butterfield/Dharmesh Shah - media
ipoSignals: Very high ARR/run-rate media signals
Large Series B at $6.6B
Enterprise/B2B pivot media signal
No public IPO filing yet
companies.nextAction: High-priority diligence: verify ARR quality, B2B/enterprise mix, retention, gross margin after model costs, app deployment depth, and Series B terms/secondary availability.

evidence.note: TechCrunch: $200M Series A led by Accel at $1.8B valuation; claimed >2.3M active users, >180K paying subscribers, $75M ARR in seven months; existing investors included 20VC, byFounders, Creandum, Hummingbird, Visionaries Club.
News clusters: $330M/€281M Series B at $6.6B valuation; CapitalG, Menlo, Nvidia/Alphabet VC arms reported; ARR/run-rate claims $400-500M need primary verification.

companies.notes: Potentially fits strategy better than pure coding if B2B all-in-one app/service platform pivot is real. Core risk: vibe-coding market crowding, retention, production-readiness, security/compliance and model-cost gross margin.
redFlags: ARR/run-rate definitions may be usage annualized not contracted ARR
Gross margin after model/cloud costs unknown
openQuestions: What portion of ARR is enterprise/B2B vs prosumer/SMB?
How many apps are deployed to production and still active after 90/180 days?
Does Lovable own backend/runtime/payment/hosting or just front-end generation?
Gross margin after model + cloud + support costs?
